foods.name: Pakhala Bhata
foods.description: A traditional fermented rice dish served with water, curd, vegetables and various side dishes across Odisha.
foods.didYouKnow: Pakhala Dibasa is celebrated every year on March 20 by Odias across the world.

foods.name: Dalma
foods.description: A nutritious preparation of lentils and seasonal vegetables cooked together with authentic Odia spices.
foods.didYouKnow: Dalma is one of the most popular dishes offered in traditional Jagannath Temple cuisine.

foods.name: Chhena Poda
foods.description: A caramelized baked cheese dessert prepared from fresh chhena, sugar and aromatic spices.
foods.didYouKnow: Chhena Poda was accidentally discovered in Nayagarh and later became Odisha's signature dessert.

foods.name: Rasabali
foods.description: Deep-fried flattened chhena patties soaked in thickened sweetened milk and flavored with cardamom.
foods.didYouKnow: Rasabali originated near the famous Baladevjew Temple and remains a cherished Odia sweet.

foods.name: Kanika
foods.description: A sweet aromatic rice preparation cooked with ghee, sugar and dry fruits as Mahaprasad.
foods.didYouKnow: Kanika is among the most loved Mahaprasad dishes served at the Jagannath Temple.

foods.name: Macha Besara
foods.description: A traditional fish curry cooked in rich mustard paste and aromatic Odia spices.
foods.didYouKnow: Mustard-based fish curries are among the most iconic delicacies of coastal Odisha.

foods.name: Chakuli Pitha
foods.description: A soft rice and black gram pancake traditionally served with dalma or ghuguni.
foods.didYouKnow: Chakuli Pitha is a staple breakfast item in many Odia households.

foods.name: Dahibara Aloodum
foods.description: A famous street delicacy combining soft dahibara, spicy aloodum and ghuguni.
foods.didYouKnow: Dahibara Aloodum from Cuttack has become one of Odisha's most famous street foods.

foods.name: Manda Pitha
foods.description: A steamed rice dumpling stuffed with coconut and jaggery filling.
foods.didYouKnow: Manda Pitha is a traditional offering during many Odia festivals.

foods.name: Arisa Pitha
foods.description: A crispy traditional sweet made from rice flour and jaggery. It is one of the most common Festival Spl. Food.
foods.didYouKnow: Arisa Pitha is commonly prepared during weddings and festive occasions.

foods.name: Chhena Jhili
foods.description: A juicy fried chhena sweet soaked in sugar syrup, known for its soft, spongy texture and rich traditional flavor.
foods.didYouKnow: Nimapada Chhena Jhili is renowned throughout Odisha. It has been prepared by local confectioners for generations.

foods.name: Bela Pana
foods.description: A refreshing summer drink prepared from bael fruit, milk and jaggery.
foods.didYouKnow: Bela Pana is traditionally consumed during Pana Sankranti. It is the best summer drink for every odia, which is good for health

foods.name: Rasagola
foods.description: Soft cottage cheese balls cooked in light sugar syrup, celebrated as one of Odisha's most iconic traditional sweets.
foods.didYouKnow: Odisha's Rasagola received the Geographical Indication (GI) tag in 2019 as Odisha Rasagola.

foods.name: Veg Besara
foods.description: A traditional Odia vegetable curry prepared with mustard paste and seasonal vegetables.
foods.didYouKnow: Besara is one of the oldest vegetarian preparations found in traditional Odia households.

foods.name: Puri Upama
foods.description: A flavorful semolina breakfast dish prepared with vegetables, spices and a distinctive Odia touch.
foods.didYouKnow: Upama is commonly served in hotels and breakfast centers across the temple city of Puri.

foods.name: Poda Pitha
foods.description: A slow-baked traditional rice cake made with jaggery, coconut and black gram.
foods.didYouKnow: Poda Pitha is one of the most loved dishes prepared during Raja Parba in Odisha.

foods.name: Badi Chura
foods.description: A rustic Odia side dish made by crushing fried lentil dumplings with garlic, onion and mustard oil.
foods.didYouKnow: Badi Chura is one of the most popular accompaniments served with Pakhala Bhata.

foods.name: Malpua
foods.description: A traditional deep-fried pancake soaked in sugar syrup and widely offered during festivals and temple rituals.
foods.didYouKnow: Malpua is a popular offering during Jagannath Temple rituals and festive celebrations.

foods.name: Tadia
foods.description: A naturally fermented palm sap beverage traditionally consumed in several tribal and rural regions of Odisha.
foods.didYouKnow: Fresh Tadia is collected early in the morning from palm trees before natural fermentation intensifies.

foods.name: Sara Papudi
foods.description: A traditional Odia sweet made from layers of thickened milk, known for its rich texture and authentic flavor.
foods.didYouKnow: Sara Papudi is especially famous in Cuttack and is considered one of Odisha's finest milk sweets.

foods.name: Kakara Pitha
foods.description: A traditional deep-fried sweet pitha stuffed with coconut and jaggery, enjoyed across Odisha during festivals.
foods.didYouKnow: Kakara Pitha is a popular offering during Manabasa Gurubara and various Odia celebrations.

foods.name: Khaja
foods.description: A crispy layered sweet prepared from refined flour and sugar syrup, widely associated with Jagannath Culture.
foods.didYouKnow: Khaja is one of the most popular dry Mahaprasad items sold around the Jagannath Temple in Puri.

foods.name: Mudi Mansa Tarkari
foods.description: A beloved Odia delicacy combining spicy mutton curry with crispy puffed rice (Mudi), creating a unique blend of flavors and textures.
foods.didYouKnow: Baripada in Mayurbhanj is famous across Odisha for its iconic Mudi-Mansa combination, especially enjoyed during festivals and local gatherings.

foods.name: Chhena Gaja
foods.description: A traditional Odia sweet made from fresh chhena, deep-fried until golden brown and coated with a layer of sugar syrup.
foods.didYouKnow: Chhena Gaja is a popular sweet offered and sold around the Jagannath Temple in Puri, known for its crispy exterior and soft interior.
